design_philosophy.core_loop: Pursuit and apprehension: drive toward fleeing criminal, maintain speed to catch them before time expires, ram vehicle repeatedly until criminal stops, then arrest
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure against distance; player must balance speed with navigation accuracy to prevent criminal escape
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed police pursuit in a Porsche 928; tactical hunting of specific named criminals across varied terrain
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game treats pursuit as racing problem, not combat problem—success hinges on driving skill and route optimization rather than weapons or combat mechanics. Road splits create navigation puzzles within action framework. Each level features a distinct criminal with themed vehicle, creating narrative scaffolding within arcade action.

difficulty.continues_system: Time limits serve as soft gating: initial 60-70 second limit to reach criminal, extended upon contact; repeated crashes or slow driving allows criminal escape
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level structure with checkpoint-like progression: completing level extends time allowance for ramming phase
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can choose route at road splits, affecting catch time; ramming frequency directly impacts time spent. Driving skill and route knowledge determine success rate.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Five sequential levels with escalating criminal profiles (Idaho Slasher → New York armed robber → Chicago pushers → L.A. kidnapper → Eastern Bloc Spy). Each level introduces new criminal and vehicle type.
replayability_hooks: Bonus point structure incentivizes no-continue completions (100,000× level vs. 10,000 base)
Time-save bonuses (5,000 per second) reward efficiency
5,000,000-point arcade trophy for full game completion
Road splits offer variable routing strategies

world_building_techniques: Radio dispatch from Nancy at Chase HQ provides mission briefing and criminal profile at level start
Named criminals with themed vehicles and regional markers (Idaho, New York, Chicago, L.A., Eastern Bloc) establish procedural narrative
Police procedural framing: player is Tony Gibson, member of 'Chase Special Investigation Department' with partner Raymond Broady
Authentic ham radio frequency display (144-148 MHz 2-meter band) grounds setting in technical realism

boss_design.philosophy: Each level features a distinct criminal as primary objective; criminal identity provides narrative motivation and vehicle variety, but mechanical challenge remains consistent: catch and ram until apprehension
notable_bosses: Ralph, the Idaho Slasher (White Lotus Esprit)
Carlos, the New York armed robber (Yellow Lamborghini Countach)

faction_indicators: Police authority: player-character is law enforcement pursuing criminals
Regional criminal networks: organized crime presence in major U.S. cities (New York, Chicago, L.A.) and international espionage (Eastern Bloc)
Chase H.Q. dispatch authority representing centralized law enforcement coordination
environmental_storytelling: Road splits and geographic branching reinforce city-scale pursuit across diverse terrain
Vehicle choice reflects criminal profile: sports cars for wealthy criminals, muscle cars for organized crime
Speed and handling differences between player Porsche 928 and escalating criminal vehicle performance
